title: Cistanthe umbellata
taxonomy: Cistanthe umbellata is classified as a member of the family Portulacaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the purslane family. It is further grouped into the genus Cistanthe, which is a group of small, annual herbs. Cistanthe umbellata is also known to be native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is part of the order Caryophyllales, which consists of various flowering plants that have similar characteristics. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among different plant species.

intro: Cistanthe umbellata, also known as Purple Owl's Clover, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and feathery foliage. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being.
